passages.passage_text:
A combination of three colours is being chosen to decorate a room. The colour must be chosen from a group of seven colours— A, B, C, D, E, F and G according to the following conditions.
I. If A or B is chosen, the other must also be chosen.
II. C and D cannot be chosen together.
III. Either C or A or both must be chosen.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following combination of colours confirms to the conditions?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If D is chosen, which of the following pair of colours must also be chosen?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If E is chosen, which of the following pair of colours could also be chosen?

solutions.solution: (c) Malls A and C earn mostly from electronics items and artificial jewellery but not from cosmetics and footwear.
(d) Mall E earns most from sports and electronics but not from cosmetics and furniture.
(b) Mall B does not earn much from ladies garments and artificial jewellery but gets maximum from footwear and furniture.
(b) Kunal is good in hockey, cricket and volleyball.
(c) Gaurav is good in baseball, cricket, volleyball and football.
(d) Sagar is good in baseball and football but not in hockey, volleyball and cricket.
(b) G is sitting second to the right of E.
(c) Except J-Engineer, in all other options, there are three persons between first and second person.
(b) The position of L with respect to the scientist is second to the right.
